observations.recommendation: We recommended the Local Chief Executive to require the City Accountant to adhere with the accounting standards provided for under IPSAS 29 in the recognition and measurement of impairment losses due to uncollectability of financial assets. Further, require the City Accountant to assess at the end of each reporting period whether there is any objective evidence that a financial asset or group of financial assets is impaired, and recognize allowance for impairment. We also recommended the reconciliation of the difference in the receivable balances between the City Accountant and City Treasurer.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive to direct the General Services Office to coordinate with the City Engineer’s Office and City Accounting Office to obtain the necessary information regarding the excluded PPE for inclusion in the annual physical count.

a. The City Engineer’s Office to issue Certificates of Final Acceptance after satisfaction of the conditions under Sec. 39.4 of the IRR of RA 9184, and to require the contractors, including the contractors specified in Annex G of this AAR, to submit sufficient warranty securities; and
b. The City Accountant to refrain from certifying the completeness and propriety of the payment of the final billing to the contractors absent such sufficient warranty securities.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive direct the BAC and GSO Offices, and other concerned offices, to submit the purchase orders, contracts, and notices of delivery within the periods prescribed under the COA Circular 2009-001 and 96-010.

We recommended that the Chief Local Executive direct the City Assessor to develop a plan outlining steps to expedite and standardize the frequency of general revisions for real property assessment and classification in accordance with Section 219 of RA No 7160 and Section 1, Chapter III of the Manual on Real Property Assessment.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive shall:

Appoint specific personnel from the City Accounting Office and City Engineering Office that will solely concentrate on the retrieving of records and reconciliation of PPE accounts; and

Upgrade the accounting system software of the city government to include the feature relevant to proper valuation and reporting of PPE accounts, if warranted.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive shall:

Require the GSO to:

Follow the procedures for the disposition of non-existing/missing PPEs provided under Paragraph 7 of COA Circular 2020-006;

Prepare IIRUP using the format prescribed under the Accounting Manuals of the respective Sectors for all PPEs found unserviceable, obsolete and/or no longer needed; and 

Include the Land, Land Improvement, Infrastructure Assets, Buildings, and Other Structures in the physical count.

Require the Accounting Office to take up the necessary accounting entries to recognize PPEs found at station, loss of PPEs, set up the corresponding receivables from the concerned accountable personnel of the missing PPEs, drop from the books of accounts the unserviceable properties based on IIRUP, and reclassify items costing ₱15,000.00 below. Further, conduct thorough reconciliation of account balances reported in the Trial Balance and Lapsing Schedule.

Require both Offices to work together to reconcile their respective property records and update each record as necessary during reconciliation.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive file the appropriate petition for money claims against the Bureau of Treasury against the fidelity bond of the deceased accountable officer Mr. Denand Lou B. Bacasmot in accordance with Section 8.5 of Treasury Circular No. 02-2019. Moving forward, we also recommended that the City Treasurer install a strict internal control measure in the handling of collections, remittances and the issuance and receipt of accountable forms to avoid unrelieved losses, shortages, misuse or improper disposition of government funds.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive shall:

a. Direct the City Accountant to deduct from the concerned contractor’s claims the amount corresponding to SPL – CSHP;

b. Direct the City Engineering Office to closely monitor the proper implementation of the Occupational Safety and Health Standards and Programs in each construction project to ensure the safety of every worker employed therein, as well as the public; and

c. Initiate the reporting of contractors found violating the safety rules and regulations to Philippine Contractors Accreditation Board (PCAB) pursuant to Section 29 of the DOLE DO No. 198, series of 2018 with which has equivalent penalties.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive shall require the concerned offices to:

c. Monitor any unutilized balances from the Special Trust Fund and revert the same after 5 years of being idle to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund, and strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive shall require the concerned offices to:

a. Maximize the utilization of the budget allocated for GAD;

b. Design plans, programs, and activities related to GAD based on thorough gender audit or analysis, or with the use of sex-disaggregated data in order to determine legitimate gender issues, in accordance with GAD-related laws, guidelines, rules, regulations, and other issuances; and

c. Strictly comply with GAD laws and related issuances.

We reiterated the previous year’s audit recommendation that the City Accountant strictly observe the use of appropriate inventory accounts for all the supplies and materials procured and subsequently recognize the related expense accounts when inventories are distributed or consumed in accordance with Paragraph 14 of the International Public Sector Accounting Standards No. 12 and Section 114 of the Manual on NGAS for LGUs.

We further reiterated the recommendation that the City Accountant require the end-users of the medicines and other medical, dental, and laboratories supplies to provide reports on utilization and other necessary information which will be the basis of the Accounting Office in determining the amount to be recognized as expense.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the City Accountant to reconcile records with the City Engineering Office (CEO) and prepare the necessary adjusting entries to transfer the completed projects to the appropriate PPE account. 

We further recommended that the CEO furnish copies of the Certificates of Completion to the City Accounting Office for proper and timely recognition of completed projects.

We recommended that the City Accountant exhaust all means to reconcile the unliquidated fund transfers between the source agencies (SAs) and the LGU, prepare adjusting entries for the reconciling items noted and require liquidation of the balances when necessary. If the sub-accounts were confirmed dormant, file a request for write-off in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 which provide the guidelines and procedures on the write-off of dormant fund transfers from National Government Agencies (NGAs)/SAs.

We recommended that the City Accountant recognize all major repairs, improvements and procurement of major spare parts as part of PPE when these extend the service life, improve the capacity and efficiency or safety of the property and/or when major spare parts are directly installed and can be used only in connection with an item of PPE in accordance with Section 17 and 19 of IPSAS 17.

Further, we recommended that the City Accountant prepare an adjusting entry to take up the understatement of the asset account and overstatement of the related expense account when necessary.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the City Social Welfare Development Officer to encourage the People’s Organizations/Associations to settle the unliquidated funds totaling ₱232,000.00 so that funds will be utilized for other sustainable livelihood programs of the LGU. We further recommended that the CSWDO ensure that specific provision on the liquidation of transferred funds be stipulated in the corresponding MOA to enforce payments from these POs/Associations as required in COA Circular No. 2007-001.

We further recommended that the Local School Board allocate sufficient funds from the SEF in the ensuing year for the full completion of the school buildings for the timely use of DepEd’s blended learning.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the City Assessor’s Office to submit the identified lacking documents for further audit evaluation of the transactions. We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure that purchased lands of the City Government are covered with owner’s Transfer Certificates of Title and other satisfactory evidence to guarantee the integrity of land ownership and safeguard against third parties and future claimants.

We recommended that Management direct the Sangguniang Panlungsod to revisit the City’s Local Revenue Code and make appropriate policies and adjustments, if any, on the stated rates for WWS operations in order not only to prevent huge amounts of arrears but also to increase collections and improve the delivery of water services.

We also recommended instructing the WWS Supervisor to reconcile the policies and charges provided in the Application Form and in the MOA and make appropriate action to collect the huge amount of arrears of water bill as of December 31, 2018 and likewise improve the quality of water services as soon as possible considering that the number of consumer applicants continues to increase every year.

We recommended that Management demand payment from the accountable officers liable for the lost property.  Instruct the Property Officer to renew the Acknowledgment Receipts for Equipment (ARE) every three (3) years to monitor the status of the property and accountability of the officer entrusted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the General Services Office to coordinate with the City Engineer’s Office and City Accounting Office to obtain the necessary information regarding the excluded PPE for inclusion in the annual physical count.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive should:

The latter should forward copies of Certificates of Completion so that appropriate adjusting entries will be prepared accordingly; and
Require the City Accountant and the City Engineer to conduct year-end reconciliation of records to ensure that completed projects for the fiscal year are closed to its appropriate asset accounts and to ensure that recorded balance of CIP only includes costs of on-going projects.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive should direct the City Accountant to make the necessary correcting entries and the latter to advise bookkeepers to carefully select the appropriate asset accounts when recording completed projects to ensure accurate financial reporting.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Require the City Accountant to classify the Other Receivables account as Current or Non-Current in accordance with IPSAS 1, paragraph 76, and ensure adequate disclosure in the Notes to Financial Statements for fair presentation. Additionally, instruct the City Accountant to reconcile the ₱4,324.00 difference in the General Ledger and Subsidiary Ledger balances and ensure the accurate recording of collections.
Direct the City Assessor to execute the appropriate Deed of Conditional Sale or Deed of Absolute Sale for beneficiaries relocated near El Salvador City National High School and provide copies to the City Accounting Office for proper recording and classification of Other Receivables.

We recommended the following to the Local Chief Executive:
Require the City Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries; and
Direct the City Engineer to assign project title corresponding to the scope of work of projects to be implemented to avoid confusion in recording transactions.

Direct the City Agriculture Office to submit the utilization reports of inventories showing actual issuances to the City Accounting Office for proper accounting of inventories issued;

Instruct the City General Service Office to regularly conduct physical count of inventories in coordination with the concerned offices and prepare the required Report on Physical Count of Inventories (RPCI) every semester to reconcile property records with accounting records and identify any discrepancies.

Direct concerned offices to identify and implement gender-responsive programs and activities that address the needs of individuals in the workplace, city, and barangays, in alignment with GAD-related laws, policies, and regulations; and
Instruct the GAD Focal Person and the City Accountant to reconcile their reports, identify the variances and make the necessary adjustments/revisions.

Assign or designate appropriate LGU personnel to assess the viability of the slaughterhouse, Seaside Food Park, and Tuburan Spring by conducting a Feasibility Study, and proceed with preparing a 5-Year Business Plan if warranted.

We have recommended that the City Accountant exhaust all means to reconcile the unliquidated fund transfers between the source agencies (SAs) and the LGU, prepare adjusting entries for the reconciling items noted and require liquidation of the balances when necessary. If the sub-accounts were confirmed dormant, file a request for write-off in accordance with COA Circular No. 2016-005 which provide the guidelines and procedures on the write-off of dormant fund transfers from National Government Agencies (NGAs)/SAs.

We have recommended that the City Accountant recognize all major repairs, improvements and procurement of major spare parts as part of PPE when these extend the service life, improve the capacity and efficiency or safety of the property and/or when major spare parts are directly installed and can be used only in connection with an item of PPE in accordance with Section 17 and 19 of IPSAS 17.

Further, we have recommended that the City Accountant prepare an adjusting entry to take up the understatement of the asset account and overstatement of the related expense account when necessary.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the City Social Welfare Development Officer to encourage the People’s Organizations/Associations to settle the unliquidated funds totaling P232,000.00 so that funds will be utilized for other sustainable livelihood programs of the LGU. We have further recommended that the CSWDO ensure that specific provision on the liquidation of transferred funds be stipulated in the corresponding MOA to enforce payments from these POs/Associations as required in COA Circular No. 2007-001.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the City Assessor’s Office to submit the identified lacking documents for further audit evaluation of the transactions. We have also recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure that purchased lands of the City Government are covered with owner’s Transfer Certificates of Title and other satisfactory evidence to guarantee the integrity of land ownership and safeguard against third parties and future claimants.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Local Housing Board (LHB) to enforce the terms and conditions of the Memorandum of Agreement by and between the LGU and the individual beneficiary of the relocation/resettlement sites and ensure that regular payments will be made by the beneficiaries to avoid the risk of default which will consequently lead to the termination of the contract agreement. We have further recommended that the LHB monitor payments made by beneficiaries and coordinate with the City Accounting Office for the appropriate posting in their respective accounts.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) to strengthen the identification of programs and projects which are gender-responsive and specifically address a particular gender issue/s and be guided with Annex D of PCW–DILG–DBM–NEDA JMC No. 2013–01 on the expenses which are allowable to be charged against the GAD funds. We have further recommended the GFPS to consider the services of gender experts/specialists for GAD-related trainings and activities in order to improve the formulation of the LGU’s annual GAD Plan and Budget.

We have also recommended the LCE, through the City Budget Office and GFPS, to discontinue the existing practice of including programs on education and indigents in the GAD Plan and Budget. Ensure that the said programs form part of its regular budget in order to carry out the mandate of the LGU in providing basic services to its constituents.

We have recommended that the City Accountant strictly observe the use of appropriate inventory accounts for all the supplies and materials procured and subsequently recognize the related expense accounts when inventories are distributed or consumed in accordance with Paragraph 14 of the International Public Sector Accounting Standards No. 12 and Section 190 of the Government Accounting Manual for LGUs, Volume I. 

We further recommended the City Accountant to require the end-users of the reports on utilization and other necessary information which will be the basis of the Accounting Office in determining the amount to be recognized as an expense.

We have recommended that the Office of the City Engineer submit the variation orders and other required supporting documents enumerated in COA Memorandum No. 2005-027 and Section 9.1.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 for further evaluation by the COA Regional Technical Services Unit prior to the conduct of final inspection of the completed project.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the City Engineering Office to give priority in the implementation of the aforementioned projects as planned and to further require the Bids and Awards Committee to expedite the procurement process of the other remaining projects.
We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive discontinue the receipt of transportation allowance (TA) and cause immediate refund for the TA received in CY 2020 totaling P96, 000.00.

We recommended that Management direct the Sangguniang Panlungsod to revisit the City’s Local Revenue Code and make appropriate policies and adjustments, if any, on the stated rates for WWS operations in order not only to prevent huge amounts of arrears but also to increase collections and improve the delivery of water services.

We also recommended instructing the WWS Supervisor to reconcile the policies and charges provided in the Application Form and in the MOA and make appropriate action to collect the huge amount of arrears of water bill as of December 31, 2018 and likewise improve the quality of water services as soon as possible considering that the number of consumer applicants continues to increase every year.

We recommended that Management demand payment from the accountable officers liable for the lost property.  Instruct the Property Officer to renew the Acknowledgment Receipts for Equipment (ARE) every three (3) years to monitor the status of the property and accountability of the officer entrusted.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:

Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit promptly the bank reconciliation statements of all funds and to prepare the necessary adjusting entries for bank charges, interest income, staled checks and unrecorded deposits to bring back the cash in bank account in its corrected balance and
Necessitate the Municipal Accounting personnel/ staff to attend training related to their duties.

observations.recommendation: 1.20	We recommended the Local Chief Executive to require the City Accountant to adhere with the accounting standards provided for under IPSAS 29 in the recognition and measurement of impairment losses due to uncollectability of financial assets. Further, require the City Accountant to assess at the end of each reporting period whether there is any objective evidence that a financial asset or group of financial assets is impaired, and recognize allowance for impairment. We also recommend the reconciliation of the difference in the receivable balances between the City Accountant and City Treasurer.

4.6	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the City Accountant to reconcile records with the City Engineering Office (CEO), prepare the necessary adjusting entries to reclassify the completed projects, and regularly monitor the CIP accounts to ensure that these are transferred to their appropriate asset accounts when these become completed and readily available.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive to direct:
The City Engineer’s Office to issue Certificates of Final Acceptance after satisfaction of the conditions under Sec. 39.4 of the IRR of RA 9184, and to require the contractors, including the contractors specified in Annex G of this AAR, to submit sufficient warranty securities; and
The City Accountant to refrain from certifying the completeness and propriety of the payment of the final billing to the contractors absent such sufficient warranty securities.

7.8	We recommended the Local Chief Executive to direct the City Engineer’s Office and/or contractors to include all the necessary details in the signboards/tarpaulins, as required by COA Circular 2013-004, of all the infrastructure projects implemented by the City Government of El Salvador.

8.7	We recommended the Local Chief Executive direct the BAC and GSO Offices, and other concerned offices, to submit the purchase orders, contracts, and notices of delivery within the periods prescribed under the COA Circular 2009-001 and 96-010.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive to:
Require the City Administrator with the help of the Head of the Human Resources Management Office to review the city’s systems and procedures, organizational structure, and staffing pattern to assess the needs and gaps, and determine the appropriate human resource complement;
Enunciate with the Sangguniang Panlungsod together with City Budget Officer the necessary appropriate action in promoting social justice and equity towards the JO workers by providing them equitable wages in accordance with the aforecited provisions of the labor code; and
Require the City Accountant and City Treasurer to take the appropriate and immediate actions in processing the payment of JO workers’ wages from cash advances to advice to debit accounts.

10.13	We recommended that the Chief Local Executive direct the City Assessor to develop a plan outlining steps to expedite and standardize the frequency of general revisions for real property assessment and classification in accordance with Section 219 of RA No 7160 and Section 1, Chapter III of the Manual on Real Property Assessment.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive to:
Direct all the twenty-nine (29) mentioned officials and employees to submit their travel authority approved by the Regional Director of DILG Region X; and
Stop the practice of disbursing all payments relating to travel expenses of the officials and employees of the LGU through the SDO, and instead require these officials and employees of LGU to be granted such cash advances under their names for their future official travels.

We recommended the Local Chief Executive:
Maximize the use of the GAD Fund to fully attain the objectives for which the funds were provided; and
Comply with the standards provided for by RA 10028 in the establishment of lactation room.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
Instruct the Acting Municipal Treasurer/disbursing officer to maintain two separate cashbooks for payroll for salaries, wages, and allowances; and other operating expenses and return unused cash advances; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to maintain separate subsidiary ledgers of cash advances for salaries, wages, allowances, and cash advances for other operating expenses.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to instruct the Municipal Treasurer/ disbursing officer to return the unused cash or to fully liquidate her cash advances at the end of the year to comply with COA Circular No. 97-002, particularly on the liquidation of cash advances.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer to:
Deposit intact all collections pursuant to Section 69 of Presidential Decree (PD) 1445 and Section 28 of COA Circular No. 92-382, dated July 3, 1992, above-mentioned regulations;
Record in the Cashbook collections and collections turned over by the Liquidating Officer as the transaction arises; and
Limit the encashment of LGU’s-own checks out of daily collections.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Direct the Liquidating Officer to verify the accountable forms issued by the collecting officer/s at the time of remittance and to accomplish the RCD in four copies to summarize the collections turned over to her by the Collectors as well as her own collections to be supported by the RCDs of Collectors including the duplicate copies of issued accountable forms; and
Require the Municipal Treasurer to submit thru the Municipal Accountant her RCDs supported with RCDs of the Liquidating Officer, RCDs of the Collectors, duplicate copies of accountable forms and validated deposit slips.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to strictly observe properly documentation on the grant and liquidation of cash advances as required in COA Circular No. 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012, re: Prescribing the Guidelines and Documentary Requirements for Common Government Transactions” to avoid suspensions and/or disallowances in audit.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to ensure that the positions for the employees responsible for the administration and training, research and planning and operations and warning are created in the regular Plantilla item and appropriately staffed, in accordance with NDRRMC, DILG, DBM, and CSC Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2014-1 dated April 4, 2014.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive through the LDRRM Council to include the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF of previous years in the LDRRMFIP and utilize the same to fund LDRRM PPAs thus:
Direct the LDRRM Officer to prepare and submit supplemental LDRRMFIP/LDRRMP for all programs, projects and activities (PPAs) to be charged from unexpended LDRRMF to LDRRMC for approval and
Require the LDRRMC to submit the approved LDRRMFIP/LDRRMP to the Municipal Development Council for deliberation and inclusion in the AIP.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Acting Municipal Treasurer to:
Exert more efforts in determining the cause of discrepancies between their records and PhilHealth's records which includes locating official receipts and other documents relative to the previous years' unpaid premiums to reconcile with PhilHealth records; and
Disclose in the Notes to Financial Statements the unpaid premiums in the total amount of P188,592.80 until resolved.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Require the BAC Secretariat to observe the submission of the perfected contracts and all its supporting documents with the duly accomplished and signed Checklist for technical evaluation of infrastructure projects within five (5) days pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular 2009-001;
Provide adequate manpower to assist the BAC Secretariat; and
Instruct the Municipal Engineer to include in his letter request for inspection the duly accomplished and signed Checklist for technical evaluation of infrastructure projects, the progress accomplishment and the additional documentary requirements for final payments.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive through the Human Resource and Management Officer, the following measures in accordance with Sections 76 and 77, Title III of RA 7160, COA-DBM Joint Circular No. 2, s. 2020:
Prioritize and consider filling up the vacant positions by hiring new employees or expediting the promotion of deserving employees to enhance its services to attain full implementation of programs, plans and activities of the Municipality for the optimal benefit of the constituents as well as to provide equal job opportunities to the community; and
Give priority to the existing Job Order workers in the appointment to the vacant positions provided that such workers meet the appropriate eligibility and other qualification requirements for the position subject to existing Civil Service law and rules.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the Municipal Accountant to:
Assess, together with other concerned personnel, the situations/problems/lapses regarding the delayed submission of reports and documents and adopt an effective and efficient method to fasten the submission thereof; and
Submit disbursement vouchers and their supporting documents within the prescribed period pursuant to Section 122 of P.D. 1445 and Section 7.2.1 (a) of COA Circular no. 2009-006.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive shall require the concerned offices to:
 
a. Shed light on the purpose and authority for the maintaining of LBP Account No. 4281-0003-10 and DBP Account No. 5-16967-811-8; and



b. If the bank accounts are still active, prepare and submit monthly bank reconciliation statements; otherwise, close the bank accounts to avoid paying dormancy fees and revert the remaining balances to the General Fund.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive shall:

a. Appoint specific personnel from the City Accounting Office and City Engineering Office that will solely concentrate on the retrieving of records and reconciliation of PPE accounts;

b. Require the City Accounting Office to maintain the lapsing schedule of the PPE accounts properly and regularly; and

c. Upgrade the accounting system software of the city government to include the feature relevant to proper valuation and reporting of PPE accounts, if warranted.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive shall:

a. Require the concerned contractor to rectify immediately the deficiencies noted and/or enforce the sanction provided for under Section 62.2.2.2 of CY 2016 IRR of 9184 in case of non-compliance of the latter with its obligations; and

b. Direct the City Engineer to strictly monitor and supervise the projects to ensure that all contractors work diligently to provide the government with quality infrastructure facilities.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive shall require the concerned offices to:

a. Revert the unutilized balances from 2016 and 2017 Special Trust Fund for DRRM to the unappropriated surplus of General Fund to be used as additional funding sources for other social services;

b. Issue PAR to establish the accountability on the 14 response vehicles in the barangays; and

c. Monitor any unutilized balances from the Special Trust Fund and revert the same after 5 years of being idle to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund, and strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the City Treasurer’s Office to ensure consistency in the imposition of the LGU’s remedies on tax collections as required in Section 2A.08(B)(5a) of the Local Revenue and the existing collection efforts of the LGU in order to continually meet its target for the ensuing years and to consequently reduce delinquent accounts of taxpayers.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the City Accounting Office, City Budget Office, City Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office, City Health Office, focal persons and all departments and personnel concerned to: (1) faithfully adhere to the pertinent provisions of the underlying guidelines on the allocation, utilization and reporting of COVID-19 funds from all sources and the proper recognition in the appropriate accounts in the LGU books; (2) comply with the rules and regulations on the grant, utilization and liquidation of transferred funds; and (3) ensure the propriety of expenditures and that disbursements are adequately supported with necessary supporting documents.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the City Engineering Office to require the contractor to expedite the completion of the school buildings in San Francisco Elementary School and Amoros Elementary School. 

We have further recommended that the Local School Board allocate sufficient funds from the SEF in the ensuing year for the full completion of the school buildings for the timely use of DepEd’s blended learning.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the City Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Office (CDRRMO) to regularly update the current year’s LDRRMF Investment Plan to include under a separate caption, the list of projects and activities to be funded from the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF (QRF and DRRMF-MOOE) of the previous years.

We have further required the CDRRMO to submit a resolution from the Sangguniang Panlungsod on the declaration of the LGU under state of calamity of the Typhoon Odette to support the payments of financial assistance to affected households totaling P446,000.00 charged against the current year’s Quick Response Fund on or before February 28, 2022.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the City Health Office (CHO) through the City Accounting Office to submit the identified lacking documents particularly on the complete list of eligible health workers (HWs) and the total number of days physically present as certified by the Human Resource and Management Officer and the CHO and other sufficient evidence on the attendance of these HWs during the periods covered to support the validity of claims.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Bids and Awards Committee and the City Engineering Office to submit the aforementioned pertinent contract documents for auditorial and technical review prior to the inspection of the completed project. 

We have further recommended that the Local Chief Executive, as the Head of the Procuring Entity, submit the required justification on the use of the Design and Build scheme on the construction of El Salvador City College.

We have recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Bids and Awards Committee -Technical Working Group to take into consideration the thorough review of the technical requirements of the winning bidder during the post-qualification stage particularly the competence and experience of the contractor’s personnel to be assigned in order to meet the expected date of completion of the project. 

We further recommend that the City Engineering Office require the contractor, MECC Builder, to expedite the completion of the projects otherwise, necessary liquidated damages will be imposed.

We have recommended that the City Accountant set-up Real Property Tax Receivable and Special Education Tax Receivable in the books of accounts at the beginning of the year based on the RPT account register/taxpayer’s index card to be provided by the City Treasurer. 

Also, we required the City Treasurer to furnish the City Accountant at the beginning of each year, with a list of real property taxpayers with the corresponding amount due and collectible for the current year as a basis for the City Accounting Office to set up the Real Property Tax Receivable and Special Education Tax Receivable.

We have also recommended that the City Government consider a policy formulation to establish criteria for the provision of a reasonable allowance for impairment losses and the subsequent recording of the same in the books of accounts particularly the delinquent accounts.

We reiterated the prior year’s audit recommendations that the Accounting Office and the General Services Office conduct periodic reconciliation between the Accounting Office and the General Services Office of the balances of PPE accounts to ensure the accuracy and reliability of accounts presented in the Financial Statements. We further required the City Accountant and the GSO to account for the reporting difference and effect necessary adjustments in the books of accounts and in the RPCPPE. 

Also, we recommended that the City Accountant maintain appropriate subsidiary ledgers containing details or breakdown of the balances of all PPE accounts.

We have recommended that the Procuring Entity (PE) submit justifications on the need for major revisions on structural designs from the original plan. Likewise, we require the PE to re-evaluate the requests for time extension that would merit the grant of a reasonable extension. Also, consider the re-computation of the corresponding liquidated damages for the delays incurred by the contractor to be deducted from the retention money.

We have recommended that the City Government discontinue the grant of financial assistance to DepEd personnel. We further required the Local School Board through the Schools Division Superintendent (SDS) to submit the underlying DepEd guidelines on the grant of research development assistance amounting to P2, 000.00 each to the 498 teaching and non-teaching personnel totaling P968, 400.00 to substantiate the payment made.

We have also recommended the DepEd Division Office to attach the prevailing DepEd policies and guidelines to substantiate the disbursements for the different programs, projects, and activities charged against the Special Education Fund.

We have recommended that the City Accounting Office and the General Services Office (GSO) institute adequate control measures and monitoring systems for all inventory items of the LGU. 

We further recommended that the GSO require all the Offices/Departments to strictly observe the proper procedure in the issuance of supplies and materials to stakeholders particularly on the use of RIS and RSMI and the maintenance of Stock Cards for proper accounting and recording of inventories in the books of accounts in compliance with Section 189 and 196 of the GAM for LGUs, Volume I.  Also, conduct regular physical counts to ensure completeness of inventories on hand to confirm the balances in the Stock Cards.

We recommended and the Management agreed to strictly adhere in submitting the copies of perfected contracts and related documentation within five (5) days upon execution in accordance with Sections 3.1.1 to 3.1.3 of COA Circular No. 2009–001 dated February 12, 2009.

We also recommended directing the City’s BAC members to coordinate with the BAC Secretariat to hasten the consolidation of the pertinent documents for submission to facilitate the review and evaluation of contracts and likewise avoid disallowances in audit.

We recommended that Management 

a. Strictly comply with Section 48 of Republic Act No. 11465 or the 2020 GAA starting January 1, 2020 (effectively of FY 2020 GAA) which provides that in no case shall deductions be made to reduce the employee’s monthly net take-home pay to an amount lower than Five Thousand Pesos (P5,000); and

b. Instruct the City Accountant to re-evaluate the deductions of the officials and employees and take out from payroll those deductions that are deemed unauthorized and would adversely reduce their net take-home pay below the mandatory threshold. Also, for those loans covered by a Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) between the banks or lending institutions and the City of El Salvador, no new loan/s shall be availed if the resulting net take-home pay falls below the threshold.

We recommended that Management direct the CESWMB members to draft the IRR of City Ordinance No. 2 in order to facilitate the proper implementation of the aforementioned ordinance.

We likewise recommended instructing the Sangguniang Panlungsod to create an office for the local ENRO.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:

a.	Require the Municipal Supply Officer to prepare and accomplish the Property Acknowledgment Receipt (PAR) and
b.	Instruct the Municipal Accountant to make adjusting entries to correct the accounting error.

We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:

a)	Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare Journal Entry Voucher taking up into account the receipt of the subject ambulance in the amount of P1,957,000.00 and the accessories upon establishment of the initial carrying value, if the costs of these accessories were not included in the stated cost of the ambulance;
b)	Require the Supply Officer to issue Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE) and Requisition and Issue Slip to the end-user to enforce accountability of the assets in their custody; and
c)	Establish policies and procedures with a clearly defined responsibility for the proper receipt, recording, and reporting of donated properties.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
a.) Direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit promptly the bank reconciliation statements of all funds and to prepare the necessary adjusting entries for bank charges, interest income, staled checks and unrecorded deposits to bring back the cash in bank account in its corrected balance.
b.) Authorize the Municipal Accountant to enroll in LBP we-access so that bank balances can be viewed, printed and can be readily available any time.
c.) Augment additional staff in the Accounting Office with accounting/ bookkeeper background to address the delay of the submission of the monthly BRS and other mandatory Financial Reports.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Direct the Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer-Designate to reconcile both their records, the subsidiary ledgers and stock cards respectively.  If the GSO does not have stock cards, require him to maintain one.
Require the General Services Officer-Designate with the Inventory Committee to complete the conduct a regular physical count on Property, Plant and Equipment once a year with the required observers such as any representative of COA and correspondingly submit the required Report of Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment (RPCPPE) to the audit team.
Likewise, require the inventory committee to observe the appropriate procedures on property management such as:
Assign property numbers to all PPE
ARE/ MR/ PAR should be renewed every three years
Physical Inventory Sticker shall be used
Infrastructure Assets should be included in the physical count

Recommendations:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to:
Create an Inventory Committee to conduct a complete physical inventory count of the LGU’s Local Road Network. A physical count of Local Road Network should be done at least once a year and prepare Report on the Physical Count of Road Network System and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor;
Direct the Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant and General Services Officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects;
Direct the General Services Officer-Designate to maintain the Local Road Inventory and Road Map, and to keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its components; and
Require the Municipal Accountant the following reporting procedures:
Prepare the Journal Voucher (JV) to record the beginning balance of the local road network and its components in the general ledger and the Local Road Network Ledger Card, respectively. Support the JV with the Registry of Public Infrastructures for Roads, working paper on the distribution of costs for the road components, and working paper on the determination of the depreciated replacement cost for road components with no available cost per registry.
Keep and maintain subsidiary records for roads and road components for every road network; and
Prepare a lapsing schedule for the computation of the depreciation for each component at the end of the year.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) Secretariat to comply with the regulations and furnish the Audit Team with the required copy of contracts along with their supporting documents within five (5) days after the perfection thereof for the timely review, evaluation and inspection of the same. Also, require the Municipal Engineer to submit request of technical inspection for all completed projects.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to require the Municipal Accountant and Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (LDRRMO) to prepare and submit a duly certified Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF within the period prescribed, which is on or before the 15th day after the end of each month, in compliance with Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to create program and activities that will intensify tax campaign and increase tax collections. Also, impose sanctions on delinquent taxpayers as provided in the Local Government Code.
Require the Municipal Accountant to record in the books of accounts the receivable balance of Real Property Tax and Special Education Tax.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer should refrain the practice of using cash advance to pay the operating expenses of the Municipality and ensure that all disbursements be issued with check directly to the name of the payees, concerned employees, and suppliers.
Also, we recommended to Local Chief Executive to make representation with the Land Bank of the Philippines (LBP) that an ATM be installed in the locality and enroll in the electronic payroll system.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant and GSO-Designate to comply with the provisions of Section 114 of the NGAS Manual for LGUs, specifically on the recording of supplies and materials as Inventories.  All purchases of supplies and materials should be taken up as Inventories except those purchases from Petty Cash Fund.
Require the Municipal Accountant that Supplies Ledger Cards (SLC) be kept updated; and the GSO-Designate to maintain Stock Cards for each inventory item received which should be reconciled with the SLC in the Accounting department.
Require the GSO-Designate to prepare and submit the Report on the Supplies and Materials Issued (RSMI) monthly to the accounting department for the recording of the utilization in the Journal Entry Voucher to adjust the Inventory accounts.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Require the Human Resource Management Office to hire additional staff in the Accounting Office to address the delay of the submission of the monthly required Financial Reports.
Direct both the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to submit above-mentioned reports within the prescribed timetable so that timely post-audit of transactions could be made and verification of the same could be facilitated.
Require the Municipal Accountant to utilize an electronic digital system to facilitate the prompt submission of reports.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management accomplish thoroughly purchase orders to provide the necessary information and basis in determining the reliability of the procurement process. Likewise, copies of purchase orders should be furnished to the auditor with copies of these documents, including the related supporting papers/attachments, within five (5) days after issuance thereof.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Require the driver to fill out Trip Tickets and the Municipal Accountant to prepare the required Monthly Reports of Official Travels at the end of each month;
Submit the related monthly report of fuel consumption and monthly report of official travels; and
c.) 	Direct the Municipal Accountant to install proper measures to control and monitor the disbursement for fuel by strict adherence to the rules and regulations provided in Sec. 4 of P.D. 1445, more specifically the provisions of COA Circular No. 77-61 which prescribes the use of the Manual of Audit for Fuel Consumption of Government Motor Vehicles.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
a.) 	Require the General Service Officer-Designate to strictly comply with the preparation of required reports enumerated in Section 4.6 of PD 1445 and COA Circular 2012-001 dated June 14, 2012.
b.) 	Direct the Municipal Accountant that complete supporting documents should be attached in the disbursement vouchers before payment shall be made, specifically the Repair History.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the Municipal Accountant to implement and comply with the regulations embodied under RA No. 11518.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive to direct the General Services Officer-Designate to maintain property cards and create a special team to design and pin appropriate labels or tag to each unit of properties and equipment of the municipal government in compliance with Section 123 of PD 1445 and COA Circular 80-124 (Part V, part 2) in order to facilitate the conduct of property inventory taking.

Recommendations:
We recommended to the Local Chief Executive the following:
Fill up the vacant MHO positions immediately and provide accordingly needed appropriation for a more effective administration and delivery of the basic rural health services needed by the constituents; and
Direct the Human Resource Management Officer to publish the vacancy of position.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to follow the rules and regulations on procurement as provided under the Government Procurement Reform Act (RA No. 9184) and its Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR), and any relevant policies issued by the Government Procurement Policy Board (GPPB) in order to prevent abuses in the procurement process.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
Direct each department/ office to prepare and submit the individual PPMP to the Budget Office for evaluation in order to ensure consistency with the Municipality's budget proposal and compliance with existing budgeting rules;
Also, instruct each department/office to forward the PPMP to the BAC Secretariat for consolidation into an APP; and
Instruct the BAC, thru its Secretariat, to provide the necessary and complete information in the APP pursuant to Section 7.3.4 of RIRR to serve as a guide in the procurement activities of the Municipality and to fast track the procurement process.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
Direct the BAC and end-user/implementing office to follow and complete first the procurement procedures set forth under RA No. 9184 and its Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR), and any relevant policies issued by the GPPB before processing the claims in order to promote transparency and accountability in the procurement activities;
Direct the BAC and end-user/implementing office to properly accomplish and fill out the PO and RFQ and prepare and comply with other necessary requirements for completeness and propriety of the transactions; and
Direct the Municipal Accountant to check, verify and ensure completeness of the supporting documents before processing the claims to avoid suspension of the transactions in audit.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor assign personnel to assist the Municipal Treasurer/Property Custodian in conducting inspection of the delivery of items purchased before the acceptance and inspection report is signed to ensure that delivery conforms to the standards and specifications stated in the Purchase Order.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the BAC to clearly specify in the BAC Resolution the mode of procurement being adopted and to follow the rules and regulations under Negotiated Procurement- in case of failed biddings pursuant to RA No. 9184 and its RIRR, and any relevant policies issued by the GPPB to promote transparency and accountability in the procurement process.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the BAC to require the supplier to submit an Omnibus Sworn Statement except for procurement project with ABC of below ₱50,000.00 pursuant to the requirement of RA No. 9184 and its RIRR and the GPPB.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
Request from the SB for an authority to charge generic-termed infrastructure projects against lump-sum appropriations under the       20% Development Fund; and
Direct the Municipal Development Council to specify in sufficient detail the projects to be funded under the 20% Development Fund so that the Municipal Mayor could implement the projects based on the timelines indicated in the plan.

Review the entries prepared by the accounting staff to ensure that proper accounts are used in recording the transactions of the Municipality;
Record to the appropriate Construction in Progress account the accumulated cost of assets which are still in the process of construction or acquisition; and
Prepare necessary adjustment on the erroneous take up on the road project payments as follows:

Direct the Municipal Engineer to consolidate all material requirements for the project and stop the practice of splitting purchase requisitions or contracts and other related transactions pursuant to COA Circular 76-41 dated July 30, 1976 and Section 54.1 of 2016 revised IRR of RA No. 9184 in order to promote effective competition and transparency in the procurement process and obtain the most advantageous price for the government; and
Advise the BAC to deliberate thoroughly the appropriate procurement modality to be adopted before the Municipality procure items in order to avoid similar scenario in its succeeding projects.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant perform her duties meticulously to avoid erroneous entries and adhere strictly on the proper classification of expenses pursuant to the Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs in order to present the accounts fairly in the financial statements.

Assign property numbers to all PPE
ARE/ MR/ PAR should be renewed every three years

To reconcile accounts between the Accounting Unit and the Property Officer from time to time
